Analyze the role of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) in achieving equitable and accessible healthcare.
Examine the challenges in implementing digital health solutions in remote areas, including digital literacy and infrastructure.
Suggest measures to ensure the effective and inclusive adoption of telemedicine services across India.
GS2.imageDescription: A doctor conducting a telemedicine consultation with a patient in a rural setting via a tablet.
GS2.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission aims to create a seamless online platform for the entire healthcare ecosystem.

GS3.headline: India's Q2 FY26 GDP Growth Projected at 6.8%, Driven by Manufacturing and Services
GS3.explainer:
• Leading economic indicators suggest India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) grew by an estimated 6.8% in the second quarter (July-September) of Financial Year 2025-26.
• The manufacturing sector witnessed robust expansion, bolstered by continued government incentives and strong domestic demand.
• The services sector, particularly IT, financial services, and tourism, maintained its growth momentum, contributing significantly to the overall economic performance.
• Agricultural output is expected to show steady growth, supported by a favorable monsoon, though global commodity price volatility remains a concern.
• While inflation remains a monitored factor, the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is cautiously optimistic about achieving its target range amidst global economic uncertainties.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.

Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Q2 FY26 covers July-September 2025.
GDP growth rate projected at 6.8%.
Key drivers: Manufacturing, Services.
RBI's role in managing inflation and growth.
Monsoon impact on agricultural output.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's economic resilience in the face of global headwinds.
Discuss the role of government policies (e.g., PLI schemes) in boosting the manufacturing sector.
Examine the challenges that India's economy might face in achieving sustained high growth, including inflation and global supply chain disruptions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of RBI's monetary policy stance in balancing growth and price stability.
GS3.imageDescription: Graph depicting India's GDP growth trend with manufacturing and services sector indicators.
GS3.trivia: India is projected to become the world's third-largest economy by 2027.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils Phase II of Project Tiger Revival Plan, Focus on Corridor Connectivity and Local Communities
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has launched the second phase of its ambitious Project Tiger revival plan, aiming for enhanced conservation efforts.
• This phase places a strong emphasis on securing vital tiger corridors to facilitate genetic exchange and prevent habitat fragmentation, crucial for long-term population viability.
• A key component involves greater engagement with local communities, including improved livelihood opportunities and participatory conservation models, to reduce human-wildlife conflict.
• Advanced technologies like AI-powered surveillance, drone monitoring, and predictive analytics will be integrated for more effective anti-poaching measures and habitat management.
• The plan also targets the restoration of degraded forest areas within and around tiger reserves to augment prey base and overall ecosystem health.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Project Tiger was launched in 1973.
Phase II focuses on corridor connectivity and community involvement.
Advanced technologies like AI and drones are part of the plan.
Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change is the nodal agency.
Aim is to reduce habitat fragmentation and human-wildlife conflict.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of tiger conservation strategies in India, highlighting the shift towards community-centric and technology-driven approaches.
Analyze the ecological and socio-economic challenges in maintaining tiger corridors and mitigating human-wildlife conflict.
Evaluate the role of local communities and indigenous knowledge in successful wildlife conservation programs.
Examine how technological advancements can be effectively deployed for biodiversity protection and anti-poaching efforts.
GS3.imageDescription: A majestic tiger walking through a dense forest, symbolizing conservation efforts.
GS3.trivia: India accounts for over 70% of the world's wild tiger population. The 'M-STrIPES' system is used for monitoring tigers in reserves.

GS3.headline: Global Digital Public Infrastructure Summit Kicks Off in Delhi, India Showcases UPI and Aadhaar Model
GS3.explainer:
• New Delhi is hosting the inaugural Global Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) Summit, bringing together policymakers, tech leaders, and international organizations.
• India is prominently showcasing its successful DPI models, including the Unified Payments Interface (UPI), Aadhaar, and DigiLocker, as examples for developing nations.
• The summit aims to foster international cooperation in building inclusive, secure, and interoperable DPIs to accelerate digital transformation and achieve Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
• Discussions are centered on governance frameworks, data privacy, cyber security challenges, and equitable access to digital services.
• Several developing countries have expressed keen interest in adopting elements of the 'India Stack' to build their own digital ecosystems.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Information Technology, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: The summit is being held in New Delhi.
India is presenting its DPI models: UPI, Aadhaar, DigiLocker.
DPI aims for inclusive digital transformation and SDGs.
Key topics include data privacy and cybersecurity.
The 'India Stack' is a reference architecture for DPI.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in achieving inclusive growth and sustainable development.
Analyze how India's 'India Stack' has revolutionized financial inclusion and public service delivery.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for developing countries in adopting and implementing DPIs, focusing on data privacy and cybersecurity concerns.
Evaluate India's role as a leader in digital public goods and its potential to foster global digital cooperation.
GS3.imageDescription: A world map with digital connections, highlighting India's digital innovation.
GS3.trivia: UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month in 2023, making it one of the world's most successful real-time payment systems.

GS3.headline: Government Reviews Progress on 500 GW Renewable Energy Target by 2030, Identifies Bottlenecks
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy held a high-level review meeting to assess the progress towards India's ambitious target of 500 GW renewable energy capacity by 2030.
• While significant strides have been made in solar and wind power installations, challenges related to land acquisition, grid integration, and financing were highlighted.
• The review emphasized the need for accelerated deployment of battery energy storage systems (BESS) and pumped hydro projects to ensure grid stability with higher renewable penetration.
• New policy measures are being considered to streamline regulatory approvals and attract further private investment into the renewable energy sector.
• India's commitment to climate change mitigation and energy security heavily relies on achieving this target, moving away from fossil fuels.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.

Ministry of New and Renewable Energy is the nodal ministry.
Challenges include land acquisition and grid integration.

mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's 500 GW renewable energy target in its climate action strategy and energy security.
Analyze the major bottlenecks hindering the accelerated deployment of renewable energy capacity in India.
Examine the role of energy storage solutions in ensuring grid stability with a high penetration of intermittent renewable energy sources.
Suggest policy recommendations to overcome financing and regulatory challenges in the renewable energy sector.
GS3.imageDescription: Solar panels at a large-scale solar farm, symbolizing renewable energy.
GS3.trivia: India's installed renewable energy capacity (excluding large hydro) crossed 125 GW by 2023.

GS3.headline: National Urban Flood Resilience Mission Launched to Combat Growing Climate Risks
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government has launched the National Urban Flood Resilience Mission, a multi-pronged initiative to address the increasing threat of urban flooding across Indian cities.
• The mission focuses on integrated urban planning, upgrading stormwater drainage systems, and promoting permeable infrastructure to enhance water absorption capacity.
• It will leverage advanced meteorological forecasting models and real-time data analytics for early warning systems and efficient disaster response.
• Capacity building for municipal bodies and community preparedness programs are integral components, aiming to create 'flood-smart' cities.
• The initiative acknowledges the severe economic and social disruption caused by recent urban floods, linking it directly to climate change impacts and unplanned urbanization.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.

Evaluate the role of good governance, community participation, and technological integration in effective urban disaster management.
Suggest sustainable urban planning strategies that can enhance the resilience of Indian cities against climate-induced disasters.
GS3.imageDescription: City street partially submerged in water, showing the impact of urban flooding.
GS3.trivia: Chennai, Mumbai, and Bengaluru are among the Indian cities most vulnerable to urban flooding.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Defence Releases New Positive Indigenisation List, Boosts Domestic Manufacturing
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence has unveiled its fifth 'Positive Indigenisation List', comprising over 100 more military systems and components that will be procured only from domestic sources.
• This move aims to further reduce India's reliance on foreign defence imports, bolster the 'Make in India' initiative, and strengthen the indigenous defence industrial base.
• The list includes critical technologies and platforms, with staggered timelines for their indigenization, encouraging private sector participation and innovation.
• The policy is expected to create significant employment opportunities, foster technological self-reliance, and enhance India's strategic autonomy.
• Industry experts welcome the clarity and long-term vision provided by these lists, urging for consistent policy support and timely procurement cycles.

Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.

GS3.imageDescription: Modern fighter jet designed and manufactured in India.
GS3.trivia: India aims to achieve a defence manufacturing turnover of $25 billion by 2025, including $5 billion in exports.

GS3.headline: National Mission on Advanced Materials Launched to Boost Indigenous Research and Manufacturing
GS3.explainer:
• The Department of Science & Technology (DST) has launched the 'National Mission on Advanced Materials' to catalyze indigenous research, development, and manufacturing in critical material science.
• The mission aims to bridge the gap between fundamental research and industrial application, focusing on areas like advanced ceramics, composites, smart materials, and materials for clean energy.
• It will foster collaboration between academic institutions, national laboratories, and industries, creating a robust ecosystem for material innovation.
• Funding mechanisms, skill development programs, and intellectual property protection are key pillars of the mission, designed to make India a leader in advanced materials.
• These materials are crucial for strategic sectors such as defence, aerospace, electronics, and renewable energy, impacting national security and economic competitiveness.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.

GS3.imageDescription: Abstract image representing molecular structures and advanced materials.
GS3.trivia: Carbon nanotubes, graphene, and high-temperature superconductors are examples of advanced materials with revolutionary potential.

GS3.headline: GST Council Convenes, Discusses Rate Rationalization and Compliance Simplification
GS3.explainer:
• The Goods and Services Tax (GST) Council held its latest meeting, addressing key issues related to rate rationalization and simplifying compliance procedures for businesses.
• Deliberations included potential adjustments to GST slabs to reduce the number of rates and make the tax structure more efficient and less complex.
• Measures to enhance technology-driven compliance, curb evasion, and facilitate input tax credit mechanisms were also on the agenda.
• States raised concerns regarding revenue implications of rate changes and sought assurances on compensation mechanisms for any potential losses.
• The Council aims to balance revenue augmentation for the government with ease of doing business for taxpayers, especially MSMEs.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.

GS3.trivia: GST was implemented in India on July 1, 2017, replacing multiple indirect taxes.
